title: gentiana clusii subsp. rochelii
intro: Gentiana clusii subsp. rochelii, also known as Gentian of the Pyrenees, is a rare and endemic subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Gentiana, native to the Pyrenees mountain range in Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Gentiana clusii subsp. rochelii include its potential to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Gentiana clusii subsp. rochelii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine in the form of dried roots and leaves.
